data.title: AI TRENDS | Genspark Launches AI Workspace 6.0 and Enters South Korea
data.description:
AI company Genspark has released AI Workspace 6.0 and officially entered the South Korean market. According to Odaily, Genspark COO Wen Shang said in Seoul on July 24 that South Korea is one of the company’s top markets and that Genspark plans to invest $100 million in the market over the next three years for team expansion, partnerships, and business development.

AI Workspace 6.0 includes a persistent memory layer called SecondBrain, the SecondBrain Note hardware device, the GenMail email AI agent, AI Design, and the GenTeam collaboration platform. SecondBrain is designed to organize information from users’ existing office tools and allow AI agents to use past projects, decisions, and communication records in later work.

SecondBrain Note is credit card-sized and can record meetings, calls, conversations, and voice notes, then convert them into searchable structured memory synced to SecondBrain. It is currently priced at $179. GenMail can learn a user’s existing emails, writing style, work relationships, and work history, and can generate reply drafts and carry out related workflows inside Gmail and Outlook.

AI Design can turn natural-language requests into visual materials and prototypes. GenTeam allows people and role-based professional AI agents to collaborate in a shared space, with AI agents in marketing, design, development, research, and other fields able to join as group chat members and work based on team memory and permissions.

data.title: Crypto Theft Suspect Dritan Kapllani Jr. Moves 126.7 ETH Again
data.description:
A blockchain data shows that crypto theft suspect Dritan Kapllani Jr. laundered 126.7 ETH again on July 22. According to Odaily, the transaction was worth about $239,000.

Earlier, on-chain detective ZachXBT said Kapllani Jr. allegedly stole crypto holders' assets through social engineering, with total losses reaching $19 million. He had also reportedly displayed luxury cars, watches, private jets, and other lifestyle content on social media, and showed $3.68 million in assets in his Exodus wallet during a Discord call on April 23, 2026.

data.title: Suspected Crypto Thief Dritan Kapllani Jr. Moved 126.7 ETH on July 22
data.description:
On-chain data shows suspected crypto thief Dritan Kapllani Jr. laundered 126.7 ETH on July 22. According to ChainCatcher, the transfer was valued at about $239,000.

ZachXBT previously reported that Dritan Kapllani Jr. was suspected of stealing crypto assets through social engineering, with the total amount involved reaching $19 million. The report also said he had long displayed luxury cars, watches, and private jets on social media, and showed $3.68 million in assets in an Exodus wallet during a Discord call on April 23, 2026.

data.title: Indonesia Seeks to Revive Cocoa Output After Years of Decline
data.description:
Indonesia is seeking to revive cocoa production after years of underinvestment, aging trees and pests have left the industry short of beans and increasingly reliant on imports, according to Bloomberg.

Once the world’s third-largest cocoa producer, the country is trying to rebuild output after a prolonged slump.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Codex Product Lead Tibo Suggests Renaming ChatGPT Work to ChatGPT Vibe
data.description: OpenAI Codex product lead Tibo posted a new idea, asking whether ChatGPT Work should be renamed ChatGPT Vibe. According to Odaily, Tibo raised the question in a post.

data.title: Pingtung Holdings Plans 10 Billion Yuan Shenzhen AI PCB Base After 9.6 Billion Yuan Placement
data.description:
Pingtung Holdings plans to invest 10 billion yuan to build an AI high-end substrate and flexible printed circuit board manufacturing base in Shenzhen, while its earlier 9.6 billion yuan private placement means the company’s monthly capital spending is nearing 20 billion yuan, according to Jiemian News. The company said the new Shenzhen third industrial park will use land acquired in Bao'an's Yanluo area in May 2026, start construction in July 2026, and begin production in 2033, with funding from internal cash and external financing. Jiemian News reported that the project is aimed at AI substrates and high-end flexible circuit boards to support the AI computing supply chain.

The placement plan announced earlier this month is earmarked for Pingtung's Qingding plant AI server and high-speed optical module HDI substrate project, which is expected to add 655,600 square meters of high-end HDI capacity. Jiemian News said the two projects together would bring the company’s total planned investment to nearly 20 billion yuan in one month. For the first quarter of 2026, Pingtung reported revenue of 7.986 billion yuan, down 1.25% year on year, and net profit attributable to shareholders of 463 million yuan, down 5.21%. The article said the company remains heavily dependent on Apple’s supply chain, while its AI server board business is still behind peers.

data.title: SEMI Raises 2026 Global Semiconductor Equipment Market Growth Forecast to 23.5%
data.description: The CSI Semiconductor Industry Select Index rose as much as 4% on July 24, 2026 and was up 2.09% at 2:14 p.m., while Southern Semiconductor ETF (159325) turned over 21.38% intraday on volume of 239 million yuan and had net inflows of 46 million yuan as of July 23. According to Jiemian News, the move came as AI computing demand surges and global advanced wafer fabrication, memory chip manufacturing and advanced packaging all enter a major expansion cycle, lifting equipment demand. International Semiconductor Industry Association (SEMI) raised its forecast for 2026 global front-end semiconductor equipment market growth to 23.5% from 16.5% and lifted the market size estimate to $152.2 billion from $133 billion. SEImingly, industry participants said pricing power in the semiconductor supply chain is shifting structurally from chip end products toward equipment and components.

data.title: Funds Buy Oil Sector Dip as Penghua Oil ETF Sees 61 Million Share Inflow
data.description:
Penghua Oil ETF (159697) saw a net intraday subscription of 61 million shares as investors bought the oil sector on the dip, while the fund last traded at 1.27 yuan at 2:00 p.m. on July 24, 2026. Jiemian News reported that since July 12, average daily traffic through the Strait has fallen to fewer than 10 tankers, delaying expectations for Middle East crude supply recovery and supporting oil prices in the short term.

BOSC Securities said short-term oil prices will remain driven by geopolitical risk, while in the medium to long term, easing conflict could leave global crude benchmarks under pressure from supply glut. It also said geopolitical shocks could still hit oil prices unexpectedly and increase volatility. The Penghua fund tracks the CSI Guosen Oil and Gas Index (399439), whose top 10 constituents as of June 30, 2026 were Jerry's Shares, PetroChina, CNOOC, Sinopec, China Merchants Shipping, Guanghui Energy, COSCO Shipping Energy Transportation, Jovo Energy, Xinao Shares and China Merchants Energy Shipping; the top 10 holdings accounted for 71%.

data.title: Prediction Markets Are Available in Eligible Regions Only
data.description: Binance Wallet announced on X that Prediction Markets are available to users in eligible regions only and may not be available for all users. The notice did not provide additional details about supported regions, access conditions, or timing. It simply stated that availability is limited based on eligibility and that some users may not be able to use the feature.

data.title: Huafeng Test & Control Raises Registered Capital 48% to 200 Million Yuan
data.description: Huafeng Test & Control recently completed a business registration change that lifted its registered capital from about 140 million yuan to about 200 million yuan, an increase of roughly 48%, according to 36Kr. The company was founded in February 1993, with Sun Qiang as its legal representative, and its business scope includes manufacturing electronic products, instruments and meters, and automatic mechanical control equipment, as well as technology development and technical services.

data.title: Iranian Media Says Two Explosions Occurred in Erbil, Iraq
data.description: According to Jin10, Fars News reported that Arab sources said two explosions occurred in Erbil, Iraq.

data.title: Nongshim, CJ Foodville, BR Korea Raise Prices Amid Cost Pressures
data.description:
According to Yonhap, South Korean food makers Nongshim Co., CJ Foodville Co. and BR Korea Co. are raising product prices as higher input costs continue to squeeze margins, with the pressure tied to a weaker won, higher oil prices and unrest in the Middle East. Nongshim said it will lift wholesale prices for products from 43 brands by an average of 5.8% starting next month, while the retail price of Shrimp Crackers is expected to rise to 1,600 won ($1.09) from 1,500 won. The company said this is its first price increase for the snack in 17 months and that packaged instant ramyun will be excluded from the hike despite accounting for 63% of sales.

CJ Foodville said it will raise the prices of bread and cake items sold at its Tous les Jours bakery chain from July 31, while leaving out popular products to limit the burden on consumers. BR Korea, the operator of Dunkin' Donuts in South Korea, said it will increase prices for 39 products by an average of 6.5% starting Aug. 2. The article also said Brent crude futures climbed above $100 per barrel Thursday as conflict in the Hormuz Strait intensified, with South Korea relying on the strait for about 95% of its oil imports from the Middle East.

data.title: CryptoQuant Analyst Says Oldest Bitcoin Holders Have Increased On-Chain Activity
data.description:
CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost said Bitcoin’s oldest holders have shown a notable increase in on-chain activity recently. According to ChainCatcher, UTXO movement among holders with 5 to 7 years and 7 to 10 years of holding periods has repeatedly exceeded 5,000 BTC per day.

Darkfost said spending UTXOs does not necessarily mean selling, as long-term holders typically move Bitcoin for two main reasons: security-related wallet address changes or asset sales. Current on-chain data does not show large amounts of funds flowing to exchanges, suggesting there is no sign of large-scale BTC selling yet.

The activity comes as Bitcoin again tests the area near $60,000, similar to market behavior seen from February to March this year. Market participants will continue watching whether long-term holder activity affects BTC supply and demand dynamics further.

data.title: CryptoQuant Analyst: Bitcoin Long-Term Holder Activity Rises as BTC Retests $60,000
data.description:
CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost said recent on-chain data shows increased activity among Bitcoin's oldest holders. According to Odaily, wallets holding BTC for 5 to 7 years and 7 to 10 years have repeatedly moved more than 5,000 BTC per day in UTXO transactions.

Darkfost said spending UTXOs does not necessarily mean selling. He said long-term holders typically move Bitcoin for two main reasons: security, such as changing wallet addresses, or asset sales. Current on-chain data has not shown large amounts of funds flowing to exchanges, and there are no signs of large-scale BTC selling by these long-term holders.

He noted that the activity comes as Bitcoin retests the area near $60,000, similar to market action seen from February to March this year. Market participants are watching whether long-term holder activity will further affect BTC supply and demand.

data.title: Poolin Files for Chapter 11 and Plans to Sell Texas Bitcoin Mining Assets
data.description:
Former global Bitcoin mining pool Poolin has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in the United States and plans to sell its Texas Bitcoin mining assets for $52 million as it winds down operations. According to ChainCatcher, Poolin and two U.S. affiliates filed voluntary petitions with the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the District of New Jersey on July 22, listing about $173 million in liabilities.

Roughly $163.7 million of that total came from unsecured IOUs issued to wallet users after withdrawals were suspended during the 2022 crypto market downturn. According to ChainCatcher, about 11,700 wallet users hold balances of more than $100. The company said its two Texas mining sites stopped mining and hosting services on July 10.

Poolin has reached an asset purchase agreement with Thor CALAP LLC. The Pyote site is valued at $15 million, while the Tarbush site’s power rights and equipment are valued at $37 million, making up the $52 million bid floor. Poolin said demand for AI infrastructure could increase the value of the sites’ power and equipment. Final recovery from the court-supervised sale will depend on bidding, expenses, and court approval.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Tencent Merges Hunyuan Multimodal and Large Language Model Units
data.description: Tencent announced that its Hunyuan multimodal model division and large language model division have been merged to form a foundation model department. According to Odaily, the new department will be managed by Tencent Chief AI Scientist Yao Shunyu to improve model research, development, and coordination and explore the intelligence limits of full-modal models.

data.title: Pentagon Lowers Iran War Death Toll To 14 From 18
data.description: According to CNBC, the Pentagon lowered its official death toll for the Iran war to 14 from 18 after the Trump administration removed four service members killed this month from the count. The Pentagon's casualty database showed seven of the 14 deaths were classified as hostile, and the website said it was updated as of Wednesday. Three military officials told The New York Times that the four service members killed this past weekend — three in Jordan and one in northern Iraq — were removed because their deaths came after President Donald Trump declared a ceasefire in April. Acting Pentagon press secretary Joel Valdez attributed the change to temporary data disruptions on the department's casualty website and said they would be fixed quickly. Rep. Adam Smith, D-Wash., said Monday that Trump had not shared Iran war casualty statistics with Congress and that there had been no transparency from the White House or Pentagon about equipment losses and casualty figures.

data.title: UK Retail Sales Beat Forecasts Again on Strong June Increase
data.description: UK retail sales rose unexpectedly strongly in June as sunny weather and the World Cup offset shopper concern over the cost of living, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | Kia Motors Shares Plunge 13% After Second-Quarter Results
data.description: Kia Motors shares fell 13% after the company released its second-quarter earnings report. According to Jin10, the stock drop came after the earnings announcement.

data.title: SK Hynix Advances P&T7 Cleanroom Start by About Three Months
data.description:
SK Hynix is accelerating construction of its P&T7 advanced semiconductor packaging plant in Cheongju and has moved up the opening of its first cleanroom by about three months. According to Odaily, industry sources said on July 23 that the company now expects the first cleanroom at P&T7 to begin operating as early as July 2027, compared with its previously announced target of October 2027.

An industry source said SK Hynix recently began bidding for utilities and infrastructure projects for the P&T7 cleanroom ahead of schedule. The source added that the company internally expects the cleanroom opening to be about one quarter earlier than originally planned. Some equipment-related bidding projects did not attract bidders and are being reissued, while all utilities and infrastructure bidding work is expected to be completed by around August at the latest.
